dismissed_findings.dismissed_reason: Auto-generated doc-sync README index file. Not command/agent prompt documentation. The ## Overview block (lines 4-8, 5 lines) contains machine-generated structured metadata (total_files, subdirectories, naming_convention) produced by posttool-doc-sync.py — not narrative explanatory prose. Per Codex feedback (issues 1-2, applied): prompt-inspector scope is limited to commands/*.md and agents/*.md; hook README index files are out-of-scope identically to non-.md implementation files.
dismissed_findings.note_on_hypothetical_severity: If erroneously treated as in-scope, the OR logic in the severity spec (critical: verbose_lines >= 200 OR verbosity_percentage >= 30%) would classify this as critical (5/15 = 33%), not minor as originally drafted. Corrected per Codex issue 4 (applied).

dismissed_findings.dismissed_reason: Auto-generated doc-sync README index file. Not command/agent prompt documentation. The ## Overview block (lines 4-7, approximately 4 lines including header and separator) contains machine-generated structured metadata produced by posttool-doc-sync.py. Per Codex feedback (issues 1-2, applied): out-of-scope identically to hooks/lib/README.md.
dismissed_findings.note_on_hypothetical_severity: If erroneously treated as in-scope, the actual section line count (header + content + separator = 4-5 lines, not 3) and OR-logic percentage threshold would yield a severity higher than minor. Corrected per Codex issue 5 (applied). Moot as file is out-of-scope.

out_of_scope.reason: Python library file. Prompt verbosity detection rules apply exclusively to *.md files under commands/ and agents/. No Markdown verbose section headers present. Module docstring (lines 2-12) enumerates safety invariants — functional code documentation, not prompt verbosity.
Python test file. Outside prompt-inspector scope. Class-level docstrings describe test suite behavior — standard pytest documentation pattern. No verbose Markdown section headers.
Shell hook stub. Outside prompt-inspector scope. Contains SAFE_COMMANDS and ASK_COMMANDS arrays with inline comments — data declarations, not verbose narrative.

Shell utility script. Outside prompt-inspector scope. 14-line header comment (lines 2-14) documents usage, output contract, and fallback strategy — functional shell utility documentation, not a Markdown ## Usage section. The '# Usage:' token is a plain shell comment, not a Markdown section header.
Shell test harness. Outside prompt-inspector scope. Inline comments label test steps — standard test script documentation.
QA verification harness. Outside prompt-inspector scope. '# ====' section separators label test blocks — standard test harness structure, not Markdown verbosity sections.
Deprecated stub. Outside prompt-inspector scope. 11-line comment block documents deprecation status, live replacement, and no-op contract — functional deprecation notice.
Deprecated stub. Outside prompt-inspector scope. 12-line comment block documents orphan status and live replacement — functional deprecation notice.

Auto-generated folder index README. Outside prompt-inspector scope — not a commands/*.md or agents/*.md file. Contains ## Overview with machine-generated stats (5 lines). Dismissed as false positive per Codex feedback.

Auto-generated folder index README. Outside prompt-inspector scope — not a commands/*.md or agents/*.md file. Contains ## Overview with machine-generated stats. Dismissed as false positive per Codex feedback.

summary.scope_note: All 10 changed files are .py or .sh implementation/test/hook files, or auto-generated README index files — none are *.md command/agent documentation under commands/ or agents/. Prompt-inspector detection rules (## Philosophy, ## Overview, ## Quality Standards, ## Safety Features, ## Helper Scripts, ## Usage Markdown section headers) do not apply to any file in this diff. The two README.md files contain auto-generated ## Overview stats blocks that match a section header pattern but are dismissed as false positives: they are machine-generated index metadata, not prompt execution documentation. Zero blocking findings.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex confirmed that FINDING A and B (hooks/lib/README.md, hooks/tests/README.md) are false positives and must be dismissed. Both files are auto-generated folder index files outside commands/*.md and agents/*.md scope. Codex also flagged that the severity calculation used OR logic (critical if verbose_lines >= 200 OR verbosity_percentage >= 30%), meaning a 33% verbosity_percentage would be critical, not minor — but the correct resolution is dismissal, not reclassification. All 5 Codex issues with PROPOSED_FIX were applied; 1 OBSERVATION_ONLY issue was logged.

findings.issue: FINDING A/B are false positives and should not be emitted as verbosity violations. hooks/lib/README.md and hooks/tests/README.md are auto-generated folder index files, not prompt execution docs. Their ## Overview blocks are structured metadata, not narrative stories.
findings.proposed_fix: Remove FINDING A and FINDING B from findings; list them under dismissed_findings as: 'Auto-generated doc-sync README index metadata; not command/agent prompt documentation; no verbosity violation.'
findings.rationale: Applied. Both files moved to dismissed_findings with detailed false-positive rationale.

findings.issue: Scope is too broad if hook README files are treated as command/agent documentation. The prompt-inspector mandate is for commands/*.md and agents/*.md.
findings.proposed_fix: In changed-files mode, scan only the intersection of changed files with commands/*.md and agents/*.md. Mark both README files as out-of-scope, same as the non-.md implementation/test files.
findings.rationale: Applied. Both README files classified as out-of-scope with explicit explanation that they are not commands/*.md or agents/*.md.

findings.issue: FINDING C is directionally correct but incomplete. It correctly dismisses non-.md files, but the corrected scope rule also excludes non-command/agent .md files.
findings.proposed_fix: Rewrite FINDING C as: 'No findings. Prompt-inspector scope is limited to Markdown command/agent prompts under commands/*.md and agents/*.md. Python, shell, test harnesses, hook stubs, utility scripts, and hook README index files are outside scope.'
findings.rationale: Applied. scope_note in summary and out_of_scope entries explicitly include the README.md files with this expanded explanation.

findings.issue: Severity calculation is wrong if A/B were kept as findings. The spec uses OR logic: critical if verbose_lines >= 200 OR verbosity_percentage >= 30%. So 5/15 = 33% would be critical, not minor.
findings.proposed_fix: Do not assign severity because A/B should be removed. If retained hypothetically, apply percentage thresholds before reporting: A would be critical.
findings.rationale: Applied. The note_on_hypothetical_severity field in dismissed_findings documents the corrected OR-logic calculation. The original 'minor' classification was wrong; corrected to 'critical (hypothetical, moot since dismissed)'.

findings.issue: FINDING B line/percentage math is inconsistent with the section-counting rule. The ## Overview section includes the header and blank separator, making actual line count higher than the 3 stat lines originally counted.
findings.proposed_fix: Do not calculate severity for B because it is out-of-scope. If retained hypothetically, recalculate using the section-counting rule.
findings.rationale: Applied. dismissed_findings note for hooks/tests/README.md documents the corrected section-line count (4-5 lines including header and separator, not 3).

findings.issue: ## Overview is listed as a critical pattern in command/agent prompt docs, not merely a warning pattern. This does not matter for these README files because they are out-of-scope.
findings.rationale: OBSERVATION_ONLY — no PROPOSED_FIX. Correct per the detection rules spec (## Overview listed under Critical patterns, not Warning patterns). Moot since both files are dismissed as out-of-scope.

codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Issues 1-5 all had PROPOSED_FIX and were applied: (1) FINDING A/B removed from findings, moved to dismissed_findings; (2) scope explicitly limited to commands/*.md and agents/*.md in out_of_scope and summary; (3) scope_note expanded to include README index files; (4) severity OR-logic corrected and documented in hypothetical note; (5) section line count corrected in dismissed note. Issue 6 was OBSERVATION_ONLY with no PROPOSED_FIX — logged, no action required.
